MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Claire Cochran, Esq. 415-580-6019 for Plaintiff.
Amy Super, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3931 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues, cites cases. Defendant does not contest the tentative ruling, responds to arguments. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's (San Francisco Department of Public Health) motion for summary judgment is granted. Even if plaintiff could show that she was subjected to an adverse employment action that materially affected the terms and conditions of her employment and a causal link existed between the protected activity and the employer's action, Plaintiff cannot establish any of her causes of action because the defendant had a legitimate reason to extend plaintiff's probation and transfer her. Plaintiff fails to present substantial responsive evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact that those purportedly adverse employment actions were the product of retaliatory animus. (See Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1042 ["If the employer produces a legitimate reason for the adverse employment action, the presumption of retaliation 'drops out of the picture,' and the burden shifts back to the employee to prove intentional retaliation."]; McRae v. Department of Corrections & Rehabilitation (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 377, 388 ["the plaintiff's burden is to prove, by competent evidence, that the employer's proffered justification is mere pretext; i.e., that the presumptively valid reason for the employer's action was in fact a coverup. . . . 'The ultimate burden of persuasion on the issue of actual discrimination remains with the plaintiff.""].) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)(see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
